completed_tasks.name: Preprint complete with N=20k data + open problems

remaining_tasks.name: PROVE C(k)=±1/2 identity analytically (should be straightforward character sum)
Express b_chi(k) exactly via L(1,chi_4)=pi/4 + digamma + 1/k corrections
Bound the 1/k correction projection onto Gram eigenvectors
If bound works: formal proof that gamma>1 for chi_4 (GRH for L(s,chi_4))

human_actions_pending.action: Get more tokens for Claude Code

decisions.decision: C(k) = sum chi_4(n)*(n mod k)/k = ±1/2 exactly for even k, ±1/2+O(1/k) for odd k
decisions.rationale: Discovered computationally, needs analytical proof (character sum identity)
Alternating sign from chi_4 creates extra smoothness

decisions.decision: Chi_4 gamma=3.07 because b_chi is nearly constant (C(k)=±1/2)
decisions.rationale: Constant projects onto top eigenvector only; 1/k corrections give fast decay

decisions.decision: Standard tools (CS, large sieve, Abel) can't prove gamma>1 alone
decisions.rationale: They don't use the eigenvector constraint Gu=lambda*u

decisions.decision: The SVD identity |<b,v>|^2 = lambda * |<sqrt(w), v~>|^2 is exact but tautological for direct proof
decisions.rationale: Need independent bound on right singular vector projection

next_action: PROVE the C(k)=±1/2 identity for chi_4 (this should be a clean character sum computation). Then express b_chi(k) exactly and bound the spectral projection of the 1/k correction terms. This is the most concrete path to proving GRH for a specific L-function.
context_notes: Session 8 was massive (18 commits). Started from dead-end Q search, pivoted to exclusion via Nyman-Beurling, discovered prime dominance + Galois correlation, measured gamma to N=20k, found the SVD identity, formalized open problems, tested Dirichlet L-functions (chi_4 has gamma=3.07), and discovered C(k)=±1/2 as the structural key. The C(k) identity is the most promising lead for an actual proof. Grok verified all numbers and called this 'the best numerical evidence for RH I have seen.' The preprint is complete at docs/gamma_preprint.tex.
